questionHtml:
Yersinia pestis is a bacterium that caused an outbreak of bubonic plague in the 14th century. It normally produces ATP in the presence of oxygen but can still produce ATP if oxygen is absent.
Which term describes this characteristic?


A.  Facultative respiration

B.  Facultative anaerobe

C.  Obligate anaerobe

D.  Obligate aerobe

meta.title: Yersinia pestis is a bacterium that caused an outbreak of bubonic plague in the 14th century. It normally produces ATP in the presence of oxygen but can still produce ATP if oxygen
breadcrumbLabels: B4.2.2. Differences between organisms that are obligate anaerobes, facultative anaerobes and obligate aerobes
